visual_goal: A clean minimal teaching stage shows specular reflection as a sequentially growing, labeled multi-bounce ray path between exactly two parallel mirrors.
critic_priorities: keep the scene limited to two mirrors, one source, and one target
make ray segments, normals, labels, and angle arcs readable
show each ray segment growing sequentially rather than appearing all at once
keep formula overlays clear and away from the zigzag path
avoid fog, sci-fi atmosphere, and decorative optical clutter

must_have: exactly two labeled mirror panels M1 and M2
labeled source S, target T, and bounce points P1 to P5
thin colored ray segments with small arrowheads
normal arrows and paired theta_i theta_r arcs at bounce points
required reflection formulas in a clear teaching overlay
must_avoid: extra walls, prisms, reflectors, or decorative objects
axes, grids, or unrelated coordinate clutter
dense laser web or all segments appearing simultaneously
formula overlay covering mirrors, rays, or labels
fog, smoke, black void, or cinematic haze
aesthetic_goals: soft reflective floor and neutral gradient background
teaching-mirror panels with colored borders and faint sheen
clear color separation among ray segments, normals, and angle arcs
blocking_conditions: a required mirror, source, target, ray path, normal, or label is missing
angle equality theta_i equals theta_r is not visually clear
multi-bounce path does not terminate at T
scene contains extra reflectors or misleading optical elements
labels or formulas are unreadable or occlude the main construction

repair_policy: remove any object beyond M1, M2, S, T, the floor, labels, formulas, and reflection helpers
replace thick beams or laser webs with thin red, orange, or yellow ray cylinders or strokes
animate each segment with bevel_factor_end so the path advances one segment at a time
reposition formula overlays to the upper side of frame when they collide with geometry
shorten and separate normal arrows, angle arcs, and labels until each bounce is legible
keep mirror materials flat and instructional rather than photoreal glass or sci-fi panels
spatial_relationships: mirror_panel_M1 and mirror_panel_M2 stand upright parallel and face each other
laser_source_S sits near the front-left side of the stage
target_sphere_T sits toward the back of the stage
bounce_points_P1_to_P5 lie directly on the two mirror surfaces in alternating order
normal_arrows_N1_to_N5 are perpendicular to their local mirror surfaces
multi_bounce_ray_path zigzags from laser_source_S through bounce_points_P1_to_P5 to target_sphere_T
formula_overlay stays outside the central mirror-and-ray construction
forbidden_abstractions: mirror panels replaced by generic glowing planes
ray path drawn as an unlabeled decorative zigzag
reflection law shown without normals or equal angle arcs
source and target omitted in favor of abstract vectors only
parallel mirrors turned into a room, corridor, prism setup, or extra reflector array
teaching labels replaced by paragraph text blocks
